parts.text: How can I get in touch?

I'd love to hear from you! Here are the best ways to reach me:

- **Email:** hello@example.com — best for detailed inquiries
- **LinkedIn:** linkedin.com/in/example — connect and message me there
- **GitHub:** github.com/example — see my code and open source work

I'm based in San Francisco (UTC-8) and typically respond within 24 hours.

**What I'm open to:**
- Full-time senior+ IC roles (frontend or full-stack)
- Technical consulting and architecture reviews
- Conference speaking and workshop facilitation
- Open source collaboration

If you're a recruiter: I read every message, but I especially appreciate notes that mention specific projects from my portfolio — it shows you've done your homework.
Are you open to remote work?

Yes — I strongly prefer remote-first teams with async communication culture. I've worked remotely for the last 5 years across multiple time zones (US and EU teams).

I find remote work makes me more productive: fewer interruptions, more deep work time, and I can structure my day around when I do my best thinking (mornings for hard problems, afternoons for reviews and meetings).

That said, I do enjoy the occasional offsite or team retreat. The best remote teams I've worked with do 2-3 in-person meetups per year for strategic planning and team bonding.

If your team is hybrid with an office in San Francisco, I'm open to that too — I just need flexibility on which days.
